review_title: A good BI solution for big data work
description: - Allowed me to query big data
author: Hasin I.
pros: Microstrategy has the ability to integrate with multiple data sources across the board from all channels of your companies data streams.
cons: It is definitely not as easy to learn as tableu. The learning curve is a little steep but people switching from tableau should face couple of weeks of challenges.

description: We have used MicroStrategy as a BI, as a reporting tool with automated file sending, as a dashboard visualizer, and as a data analytic tool, giving insanely useful answers in each of those roles.
author: Austin M.
pros: MicroStrategy is extremely easy to use, the impact it has on the routine is insane, helps you make decisions at really high speed and gives you the information you need when you need it the most.
cons: I think MicroStrategy web could be improved to have a lot more features than it has, it pales in comparison to the architect desktop version, personally, this makes it portable and awesome.

description: We use Microstrategy to create demonstration dashboards for our our analytics software. They have a fairly robust user community and they are getting better with each iteration. Beyond the way one has to manipulate the frames and maps, the software is powerful and useful for creating data visualization dashboards.
author: Bill T.
pros: The software is easy to install and get up and running. It handles a wide variety of data types and has many options for displaying data. They now have a compilable version that lets developers hard code the dashboards to work with input software which is nice (although a little pricey for smaller applications).
cons: The user interface was less than intuitive, development support was not strong, and some of the features do not respond as expected. Specifically, maps do not appear to scale properly - I wound up with a much larger map (the entire northern hemisphere) than wanted (the continental USA) and could never get them to display properly unless I removed the data element and started over. Setting up dashboard frames to fit various screen sizes is a little complicated. Setting up “reusable frames” was a little difficult to master, because of the sidebar configuration tools.

description: It is a high-performance platform that allows you to efficiently import data from different sources to facilitate its analysis and improve decision-making.
author: Nicolas P.
pros: It has a nice and intuitive user interface. It makes it easy to transform large amounts of data into intuitive dashboards. It allows generating dashboards with visually very pleasant graphics. It is very easy to create report based on data.
cons: Nothing negative that I can mention about this robust and versatile platform

review_title: Great Tool For Making Analytics And Reporting
description: It is really really very good software where we can customise alot of stuff with single tool
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This software is very easy to install and ready to use. It has complete full of great features to use. It is very good software for reporting and analysis and also it always produce actionable, insightful and informative .
cons: The pricing is little expensive as comparison to others, need good amount of information .

review_title: Solid BI tool for enterprise wide reporting but resource intensive (both cost and initial setup)
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - Full of advanced features like data storage in MicroStrategy cubes for faster reporting times, advanced grouping and filtering, ability to write your own SQL in the backend, ability to customize using SDK interface - MicroStrategy Mobile is game changer. Great app and makes a solid impression on C-suite executives
cons: - Initial setup takes time (If you compare with Tableau, etc, the setup of MicroStrategy is much more time intensive). Have to setup all attributes and measures beforehand.
author_profession: Manager of Solution Delivery, Marketing & Sales Ventures

review_title: Platform to evaluate BI into a single tool
author: Ankit G.
pros: In our organization we're using this tool to perform ETL by cleansing and organizing the structured and non-structured data and creating visualization reports out of it
cons: This tool does gets slow in performing various functions as huge datasets enter into the system, but I hope this can be improved in the future.

author: Sean M.
pros: I enjoy having the ability to run custom reports that help me understand how my staff is behaving on the phones. Exporting that data is a breeze, overall a great product.
cons: It can be intimidating for new users, better integration of help functions would be appreciated.

description: My company previously used tableau as our BI tools. Tableau is impressive in building nice and interactive visualization but not so much on keeping the structure of the data which can lead to data integrity issues. Microstrategy in the beginning was behind in visualization but over time the development has improved significantly in many areas. With microstrategy, the final product can be fully trusted and consistent across the board.
author: Felix S.
pros: Evolving periodically for better. Really good in maintaining data structures and hierarchy to ensure data integrity and consistency Capable to do almost anything that other competitors have. Hyper intelligence is a game changer in BI world.
cons: 1. Setup can be complicated and difficult since involved many parties (driver vendor, database vendor, etc) due to compatibility issues. 2. Prefer inner join, less support for outer join. 3. Relationship and data structures development can take lots of time and thought.

review_title: MicroStrategy tool was helpful for me providing daily, monthly, and quarterly reporting to directo
author: Sri Ramani B.
pros: Scalable MicroStrategy was a helpful tool for providing daily, monthly, and quarterly reporting due to the ability to build automated reports that can be sent to large groups of people using distribution lists.,data from multiple SOR sources in the organization helped in monitoring our SLA management. security features to allow the report to be used by multiple roles and levelsMobile functionality
cons: users not having ability to make changes to metrics and attributes without having to rely to heavily on MicroStrategy developers, it would make the product a great dea and more useful.

review_title: My company utilized MicroStrategy to provide Automotive Dealers with reporting dashboards.
author: Nathan H.
pros: The reliability was key. We had to refresh data weekly for over 2,000 dealerships and had many thousands of users, so it was important the dashboards were working 100% of the time.
cons: It was difficult for our team to make updates to the dashboards when requested by our clients. Also, custom reports were a bit dofficult to create and schedule. Still a good reporting product, though, and a great value for the money because you can display all sorts of data in different ways (bar chart, pie chart, trend lines).

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We use microstrategy to create custom dashboards for internal use and for our customers. We are able to create SSO from our customer portal to microstrategy to allow our customers to have access to their dashboards. With microstrategy you are able to provide summary level dashboards with links that allow you to click to drill down to receive further details. We are also able to provide links so the user can export the reports to excel so they can further filter the information as needed. The creation of cubes enables us to have the data calculations ready and available to reduce the response time to present dashboards to the end user.
cons: Our customers would like to be able to subscribe to receive scheduled emails of their dashboards versus having to login to our portal to view the information. We have not be able to figure out how to provide them this feature.

review_title: Very powerful data gathering, takes awhile to master
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love how powerful this program is. You can gather data from different applications and compile it all into microstrategy. You can then organize and display the data in many ways to fit your needs.
cons: This software is not very user friendly. There are many different attributes you can assign but the definitions and understanding on how each behaves is difficult to master.

review_title: I liked using the system but it was hard to always trust the data
description: Really liked the ease of use.
author: Tim B.
pros: It was easy to use and create adhoc reports. I was able to send out reports to my clients or those who I supported to give them a good look of their business.
cons: It was hard to trust the data. When you can't trust the data then anything the software can do becomes mute. It was partly our fault but didn't love that aspect.

description: I'm quite satisfied with the tool due to the abundance of features and quality.
author: Leszek B.
pros: Many interesting features available. Nice visualisation.
cons: The performance of data loading is sometimes quite long.

author: Josh J.
pros: It is a professional tool that helps bring to light analytics.
cons: It is more expensive than competitors in the field.

author: Mohammed S.
pros: It has some good graphs, and you can add your own ones. Intelligent Cubes are the only unique feature I've seen it in this BI tool. It has a good community.
cons: Dealing with SQL queries, and Microsoft Access was difficult, and probably result in an error. Even though the query was 100% correct. It generates wrong values for the query I used. I think I prefer another tool.
author_industry: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I've been testing the latest version 10 and it is really good, it has new features to create dashboards in an easy and quickly way more similar to what the market for self service BI is requiring
author: Juan N.
pros: Good design, easy to use, development is really fast letting you select the data and object that you want to visualize and therefore just create a dashboard within seconds.
cons: The best is to have a well designed data model first or a data warehouse to pull the data and just do some of the latest transformation with the tool, that way you can get the most of it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Within the e-commerce space we rely on MicroStrategy Analytics (especially within the Flex OLAP space) for our complex reporting asks. A feature that is really useful is when you are pulling a very large amount of data, you can add it to a queue and it will continue to process while you are working through other tasks. Other programs tend to crash or glitch when trying to do the same.
author: Tami S.
pros: No matter how complex the data pull, I can create & save custom dashboards so quick & easy data pulls that I am able to share with my team and other collaboration departments. Exporting data is exponentially faster than with other, similar software programs we have access to.
cons: If you're unfamiliar with MicroStrategy the interfaces can be so overwhelming. I personally do not find the interfaces very intuitive either. Luckily there are tons of resources for walking users through pretty much every scenario you can imagine. And with a little time it becomes second nature.

description: Great tool for ad-hoc and standard reporting purposes as after export you don't need to spend time on formatting in excel.
author: Szilárd K.
pros: -hierarchy is definable, and drilling up and down within the data set is good -it has a great number of functions -a nice tool for automating reporting
cons: - with the several menu bars you have go get used to it -can get slow when asking for too much data

description: MicroStrategy is best tool to use if you are working or looking for the data with too much security.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: MicroStrategy is the best BI tool used for reporting the data in very good design and graphical way. The best part I liked the most about the tool is we can have a different kind of security which can be implemented like the object kevel security, data level security or folder level. So the organizations basically dealing with banks data or sales data and they want there competitor vendor doesn't need to show that data, we can easily restrict. Wherein the other tools also we can do so but not but creating a single report. We might need to create multiple reports.
cons: I wouldn't say anything is least in the product as you can do anything within the tool what business need is. So basically if you know sql all requirement's will be fulfilled. Secondly, Some thinks its cost is more compared to other tools available in market so yes its high because the functionality it provides makes it costlier.

review_title: MicroStrategy is a Powerful BI Tool - Stakeholders are Delighted!
description: Overall, I've been extremely happy with the tool. It is wildly flexible in terms of web/mobile/printability. Great product.
author: Curt J.
pros: I love MicroStrategy's flexibility in providing both interactive visualizations as well as printable reports. (Yes, some people still need things printed... and a lot of competitors refuse to acknowledge this.)
cons: There is certainly a learning curve in figuring out the idiosyncrasies of MicroStrategy, but that's the case with any product.

description: I analyze merchandise levels for a buying office for a very large retailer. We need to look at our data from every imaginable angle and my job would be impossible without MicroStrategy. One of the most useful tools within it is being able to look at product views, add to cart rate & conversion metrics, and compare to inventory levels.
author: Nolan H.
pros: As a merch analyst in a buying office, I need to create my own custom reporting I love how flexible MicroStrategy is in that regard. I have almost 20 custom dashboards saved that I can pull & export as needed, and share with the rest of my working group as well as other departments. You can drill down any metric as far as you want, or step back and get a great big picture view of things like sales, demand, styles sold etc. and when you export you can choose the format as well (excel & PDF are my most commonly used options)
cons: The interface is very dated looking, I feel transported back to 1996 whenever I open up MicroStrategy. It really has no impact on using the product, but a more modern look would be appreciated.
